channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: New KadNap botnet hijacks ASUS routers to fuel cybercrime proxy network
item.description: A newly discovered botnet malware called KadNap is targeting ASUS routers and other edge networking devices to turn them into proxies for malicious traffic. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The New Turing Test: How Threats Use Geometry to Prove 'Humanness'
item.description: Malware is evolving to evade sandboxes by pretending to be a real human behind the keyboard. The Picus Red Report 2026 shows 80% of top attacker techniques now focus on evasion and persistence, includ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'BlackSanta' EDR Killer Targets HR Workflows
item.description: A campaign by Russian-speaking cyberattackers hijacks workflows to deliver security-busting malware, allowing attackers to steal data without detection....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New "LeakyLooker" Flaws in Google Looker Studio Could Enable Cross-Tenant SQL Queries
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed nine cross-tenant vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio that could have permitted attackers to run arbitrary SQL queries on victims' databases and exfiltrate...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: How to Stop AI Data Leaks: A Webinar Guide to Auditing Modern Agentic Workflows
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a tool we talk to; it is a tool that does things for us. These are called AI Agents. They can send emails, move data, and even manage software on their o...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA: Recently patched Ivanti EPM flaw now actively exploited
item.description: CISA flagged a high-severity Ivanti Endpoint Manager (EPM) vulnerability as actively exploited in attacks and ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch systems within three weeks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Zero-Day Scramble is Avoidable: A Guide to Attack Surface Reduction
item.description: You can't control when the next critical vulnerability drops. You can control how much of your environment is exposed when it does. The problem is that most teams have more internet-facing exposure th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: APT28 Uses BEARDSHELL and COVENANT Malware to Spy on Ukrainian Military
item.description: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group tracked as APT28 has been observed using a pair of implants dubbed BEARDSHELL and COVENANT to facilitate long‑term surveillance of Ukrainian military personne...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft to enable Windows hotpatch security updates by default
item.description: Microsoft will turn on hotpatch security updates by default for all eligible Windows devices managed through Microsoft Intune and the Microsoft Graph API, beginning with the May 2026 Windows security ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: APT28 hackers deploy customized variant of Covenant open-source tool
item.description: The Russian state-sponsored APT28 threat group is using a custom variant of the open-source Covenant post-exploitation framework for long-term espionage operations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Threat Actors Mass-Scan Salesforce Experience Cloud via Modified AuraInspector Tool
item.description: Salesforce has warned of an increase in threat actor activity that's aimed at exploiting misconfigurations in publicly accessible Experience Cloud sites by making use of a customized version of an ope...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Flags SolarWinds, Ivanti, and Workspace One Vulnerabilities as Actively Exploited
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added three security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. Th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Teams phishing targets employees with A0Backdoor malware
item.description: Hackers contacted employees at financial and healthcare organizations over Microsoft Teams to trick them into granting remote access through Quick Assist and deploy a new piece of malware called A0Bac...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google: Cloud attacks exploit flaws more than weak credentials
item.description: Hackers are increasingly exploiting newly disclosed vulnerabilities in third-party software to gain initial access to cloud environments, with the window for attacks shrinking from weeks to just days....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Dutch govt warns of Signal, WhatsApp account hijacking attacks
item.description: Russian state-sponsored hackers have been linked to an ongoing Signal and WhatsApp phishing campaign targeting government officials, military personnel, and journalists to gain access to sensitive mes...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: White House Cyber Strategy Prioritizes Offense
item.description: In a seven-page strategy document, the Trump administration signaled a shift to preemption and deterrence to handling cyber threats....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 'InstallFix' Attacks Spread Fake Claude Code Sites
item.description: A fresh cyberattack campaign blends malvertising with a ClickFix-style technique that highlights risky behavior with AI coding assistants and command-line interfaces....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Are We Ready for Auto Remediation With Agentic AI?
item.description: With the rapid innovations in AI, we are entering an exciting era of automated risk remediation. Learn about security team readiness to leverage agentic AI for threat and exposure management....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Ericsson US discloses data breach after service provider hack
item.description: Ericsson Inc., the U.S. subsidiary of Swedish networking and telecommunications giant Ericsson, says attackers have stolen data belonging to over 15,000 employees and customers after hacking one of it...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Malicious npm Package Posing as OpenClaw Installer Deploys RAT, Steals macOS Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious npm package that masquerades as an OpenClaw installer to deploy a remote access trojan (RAT) and steal sensitive data from compromised hosts. The ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Teams will tag third-party bots trying to join meetings
item.description: Microsoft says Teams will soon automatically tag third-party bots in lobbies, allowing organizers to control whether they can join meetings. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ShinyHunters claims ongoing Salesforce Aura data theft attacks
item.description: Salesforce is warning customers that hackers are targeting websites with misconfigured Experience Cloud platforms that give guest users access to more data than intended. However, the ShinyHunters ext...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI warns of phishing attacks impersonating US city, county officials
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warns that criminals are impersonating U.S. officials in phishing attacks targeting businesses and individuals who request city and county planning and zoning...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UNC4899 Breached Crypto Firm After Developer AirDropped Trojanized File to Work Device
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as UNC4899 is suspected to be behind a sophisticated cloud compromise campaign targeting a cryptocurrency organization in 2025 to steal millions of dollars in crypt...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Why Password Audits Miss the Accounts Attackers Actually Want
item.description: Password audits often focus on complexity rules but miss the accounts attackers actually target. Specops Software explains how breached passwords, orphaned users, and service accounts can leave organi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft still working to fix Windows Explorer white flashes
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed that it's still working to fully address a known issue that causes bright white flashes when opening the File Explorer on some Windows 11 systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware
item.description: Another week in cybersecurity. Another week of "you've got to be kidding me." Attackers were busy. Defenders were busy. And somewhere in the middle, a whole lot of people had a very bad Monday morning...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Chinese Cyber Threat Lurks In Critical Asian Sectors for Years
item.description: An undefined Chinese-speaking actor wields a combo of custom malware, open source tools, and LOTL binaries against Windows and Linux, likely for spying....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Can the Security Platform Finally Deliver for the Mid-Market?
item.description: Mid-market organizations are constantly striving to achieve security levels on a par with their enterprise peers. With heightened awareness of supply chain attacks, your customers and business partner...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Chrome Extension Turns Malicious After Ownership Transfer, Enabling Code Injection and Data Theft
item.description: Two Google Chrome extensions have turned malicious after what appears to be a case of ownership transfer, offering attackers a way to push malware to downstream customers, inject arbitrary code, and h...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
